old_exact: This Court has defined a good faith purchaser as follows: the facts which would render Good faith is "an honest intention to abstain from taking any unconscientious advantage of another even through the forms or technicalities of law, together with an absence of all information transaction or belief of unconscientious."
new_exact: This Court has defined a good faith purchaser as follows: Good faith is "an honest intention to abstain from taking any unconscientious advantage of another even through the forms or technicalities of law, together with an absence of all information or belief of facts which would render the transaction unconscientious."
source_quote:
This Court has defined a good faith purchaser as follows:

             Good faith is "an honest intention to abstain from taking
      any unconscientious advantage of another even through the forms
      or technicalities of law, together with an absence of all information
      or belief of facts which would render the transaction
      unconscientious." N.D.C.C. § 1-01-21.
evidence: The DB text in paragraph [¶16] contains a corrupted definition of 'good faith purchaser'. The DB version includes extraneous text ('the facts which would render') before the definition and contains a malformed phrase ('absence of all information transaction or belief of unconscientious') that breaks the grammatical structure. The PDF source shows the correct version: the prefix phrase is removed and the complete definition reads properly as 'absence of all information or belief of facts which would render the transaction unconscientious.' The error appears to be an OCR or text extraction defect that scrambled the text during ingestion.
note: This is paragraph [¶16], which is a block quotation of legal definitions from N.D.C.C. § 1-01-21. The error corrupts the Court's citation and definition, introducing false text and removing essential text. High confidence because the source text is clear and the difference is substantial and unambiguous.

source_quote:
interest in real property. Attached as an exhibit to the affidavit was a type-
written document entitled "Warranty Deed." The document purportedly
evidence: The PDF source (pdftotext output) shows 'type-' on line 69 ending with a hyphen at end-of-line, and 'written' on line 70, indicating a standard hyphenated word broken across a page boundary. The raw bytes confirm: 74 79 70 65 2d 0a 77 72 69 74 74 65 6e (type-\nwritten). The DB text incorrectly introduced a space after the hyphen ('type- written'), which is an ingestion error. The correct form is 'type-written' (the hyphenated compound joined without the space that appeared during PDF-to-text conversion). This occurs in [¶3] describing the document alleged to convey real property, at byte position 2285 in the DB text.
note: OCR/ingestion artifact: a space was incorrectly inserted when the PDF's line-break hyphen was processed. The source PDF correctly shows the hyphenated word split across lines (type- / written), which should be reconstructed as the compound word 'type-written' without the erroneous space. Byte-exact count verified as 1 occurrence in entire DB text.
